fields.description:
## Description

**What**: A shared shell script (confluence-fetch.sh) that handles URL fetching with Confluence authentication, file-based caching, and config-driven reference URL management.
**Why**: All subsequent integration stories (researcher sub-agent and citation injection) depend on a single, tested fetch-and-cache layer. Centralizing auth and cache logic prevents duplication and makes credential handling reviewable in one place.
**Scope**:
- IN: confluence-fetch.sh — fetches a given URL; detects whether it is a Confluence URL by exact domain match against confluence.base_url in dso-config.conf; uses CONFLUENCE_EMAIL+CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN env vars for Basic Auth on Confluence URLs; public URLs fetched without auth
- IN: Tiny Confluence link resolution — URLs matching /wiki/x/<base64> followed via `curl --location-trusted=false -L` (strips Authorization header on redirect to non-base_url domains) before page ID extraction
- IN: /tmp/dso-ref-cache/ directory with 1-hour mtime-based TTL; cached files stored with 0600 permissions; cache hit emits "[cache hit: <url>]" to stderr without making a network request; concurrent writes serialized via flock
- IN: Missing CONFLUENCE_EMAIL or CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN — Confluence URLs skipped with stderr warning; public URLs continue unaffected
- IN: Domain guard FAILS CLOSED — unset confluence.base_url rejects all Confluence-authenticated URLs (never all-accept); trailing-slash base_url normalized before domain comparison
- IN: Startup credential-placement check — rejects CONFLUENCE_EMAIL or CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN found in dso-config.conf with a security warning (credentials MUST be env vars)
- IN: dso-config.conf config keys: confluence.base_url, confluence.reference.url, confluence.reference.tags (per-URL; multiple entries supported)
- OUT: CQL search, citation injection, roadmap integration (those are separate stories)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, confluence-fetch.sh fetches any URL passed to it; Confluence-domain URLs use Basic Auth via CONFLUENCE_EMAIL+CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN; public URLs require no credentials
  ← Satisfies: "Reference documents (Confluence-authenticated and public URLs) are fetched and stored in /tmp/dso-ref-cache/ with a 1-hour mtime-based TTL"
- When this story is complete, fetched content is cached in /tmp/dso-ref-cache/ with 0600 permissions; a second call within the 1-hour TTL window serves from disk and emits "[cache hit: <url>]" to stderr without a network request; concurrent writes to the same cache file are serialized via flock
  ← Satisfies: "Repeated calls within the cache window serve content from disk and emit a '[cache hit: <url>]' message to stderr without making any network request"
- When this story is complete, missing CONFLUENCE_EMAIL or CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN causes Confluence URLs to be skipped with a stderr warning; all other processing continues uninterrupted
  ← Satisfies: "When CONFLUENCE_EMAIL or CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN is not configured, Confluence-authenticated URLs are skipped with a stderr warning"
- When this story is complete, the domain guard FAILS CLOSED: unset confluence.base_url causes confluence-fetch.sh to reject all Confluence-authenticated URLs (never all-accept fallback); trailing-slash base_url is normalized at config read so domain comparison matches URLs without trailing slash
  ← Satisfies: SC6 (domain guard FAIL CLOSED behavior for unset base_url and trailing-slash normalization)
- When this story is complete, tiny Confluence links (/wiki/x/<base64>) are resolved by following `curl --location-trusted=false -L` redirects; Authorization header is stripped on any redirect to a non-base_url domain to prevent credential exfiltration via attacker-controlled tiny-link redirects
  ← Satisfies: SC6 (tiny-link handling with off-domain redirect protection)
- When this story is complete, startup credential-placement check rejects CONFLUENCE_EMAIL or CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN found in dso-config.conf with a clear security warning directing users to use environment variables; the script aborts with non-zero exit rather than proceeding with compromised credentials
  ← Satisfies: SC3 (credentials MUST be env vars; startup rejection on dso-config.conf placement)
- When this story is complete, reference URL list and per-URL topic tags are parseable from dso-config.conf (confluence.reference.url / confluence.reference.tags key pairs) without modifying plugin code
  ← Satisfies: "The reference URL list is configurable in dso-config.conf with per-URL topic tags; adding a new reference requires only a config change with no modification to plugin code"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic, including (i) unset base_url rejection, (ii) trailing-slash base_url normalization, (iii) off-base_url redirect during tiny-link resolution stripping Authorization, (iv) credential-in-dso-config.conf startup rejection

## Considerations

- [Security] curl error output may expose CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN in stderr — use --stderr /dev/null or token masking before the curl invocation to prevent token leakage in CI logs and session transcripts
- [Security] Domain guard FAILS CLOSED: unset confluence.base_url rejects all Confluence URLs (never all-accept) — prevents credential exfiltration when base_url is missing and credentials are set
- [Security] `curl --location-trusted=false -L` ensures Authorization header is stripped on off-domain redirects during tiny-link resolution
- [Reliability] /tmp write access unavailable on hardened CI runners — this is a known limitation; DSO_REF_CACHE_DIR override is out of scope for this story; document in KNOWN-ISSUES.md (Story F)
- [Reliability] Concurrent calls to confluence-fetch.sh may produce partially-written cache files — implement file locking (flock) or atomic write-to-temp-then-rename to prevent cache corruption under concurrent implementation-plan sessions
- [Maintainability] all URL fetching in dso:confluence-researcher and ref-library-loader.sh must call through confluence-fetch.sh — do not duplicate auth logic in either consumer
- [Integration] Verified: Basic Auth via curl -u email:token is confirmed working for Confluence Cloud REST API (atlassian-python-api, aashari/mcp-server-atlassian-confluence, coulibal/Confluence-Rest-API)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
